# Generated Files

This repo intentionally checks in generated ABI and eBPF artifacts so normal
builds do not need to compile BPF at runtime. Edit the source-of-truth inputs,
run the generator command, and commit the generated diff.

## Source Inputs

| Input | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `internal/abi/abi.yaml` | Single source of truth for storage schema, schema docs, tracepoint metadata, and kernel-event wire layout |
| `internal/abi/gen/` | Pure-Go generator that compiles `abi.yaml` into Go and C outputs |
| `internal/abi/gen.go` | `go generate` entrypoint for ABI/schema generation |
| `internal/ebpf/collector.bpf.c` | BPF programs, maps, filters, and event emission |
| `internal/ebpf/gen.go` | `bpf2go` invocation for compiling the BPF object and Go bindings |

## Generated Outputs

| Output | Generated by | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `internal/abi/kernel_event.gen.h` | `make generate` | C event-layout header included by `collector.bpf.c` |
| `internal/abi/schema_gen.go` | `make generate` | `abi.SchemaVersion` and `abi.SchemaDoc` |
| `internal/abi/tracepoints_gen.go` | `make generate` | Runtime tracepoint attachment table |
| `internal/storage/schema_gen.go` | `make generate` | SQLite DDL and storage column lists |
| `internal/ebpf/collector_bpfel.go` | `make ebpf` | Go bindings generated by `bpf2go` |
| `internal/ebpf/collector_bpfel.o` | `make ebpf` | Embedded BPF ELF object |

Do not hand-edit generated outputs. The ABI-generated files carry embedded
content hashes, and tests verify those hashes to catch manual edits.

## Commands

```bash
make generate          # pure Go; updates ABI/schema generated files
make verify-generated  # verifies ABI-generated file hashes without rebuilding
make ebpf              # Linux/CI; runs generate, then bpf2go/clang
go test ./...          # includes generated-file consistency tests
```

Use `make generate` after changing `internal/abi/abi.yaml`.

Use `make ebpf` after changing `internal/ebpf/collector.bpf.c`, or after an
ABI change that affects tracepoints or the kernel-event layout. `make ebpf`
requires clang with a working BPF target; Apple clang cannot build the checked-in
BPF object.

## Transport Terminology

The current kernel-to-userspace transport is:

```text
BPF_MAP_TYPE_RINGBUF -> bpf_ringbuf_reserve -> bpf_ringbuf_submit -> ringbuf.NewReader
```

The loader-visible map is a BPF ring buffer. Failed ring-buffer reservations are
counted in the `ringbuf_drops` per-CPU map; userspace samples the counter and
attributes deltas to `dropped_before`.

If the transport changes away from `BPF_MAP_TYPE_RINGBUF`, update:

- `internal/ebpf/collector.bpf.c`
- `internal/ebpf/collector_linux.go`
- `docs/abi/capture.md`
- this document
- tests that assert the collector object and loader contract
